content:
First, introduce notation:

• Let \(O\) be the center of the large circle \(C_2\), with radius \(R=9\).  
• Let \(O_1\) be the center of the smaller circle \(C_1\), with unknown radius \(r\).  Since \(C_1\) is internally tangent to \(C_2\) at \(A\), we have  
\[
OO_1=R-r=9-r,
\]
and \(A\) lies on the line \(OO_1\) at distance 9 from \(O\).  We may place
\[
O=(0,0),\quad O_1=(9-r,0),\quad A=(9,0).
\]

---

1.  Point \(P\) is on \(C_1\) and the tangent to \(C_1\) at \(P\) passes through \(O\).  
   Equivalently, \(OP\) is a tangent from \(O\) to \(C_1\), so by the tangent–secant (or power) relation
\[
(OP)^2 = (OO_1)^2 - r^2 = (9 - r)^2 - r^2 = 81 - 18r.
\]
Set
\[
t \;=\; OP \;=\;\sqrt{81-18r}\;.
\]

2.  The ray \(OP\) meets the big circle \(C_2\) again at \(Q\).  Since \(OQ=9\), the points on the ray are in order
\[
O\;(0)\quad P\;(t)\quad Q\;(9)\quad\text{(all distances from \(O\) along }OP\text{)}.
\]
Hence
\[
PQ=\,9-t\,.
\]

3.  The tangent to \(C_1\) at \(A\) is perpendicular to \(O_1A\), i.e.\ perpendicular to the \(x\)-axis, so it is the vertical line \(x=9\).  This line meets the ray \(OP\) at a point \(R=(9,y_R)\).  One finds by similar‐triangles or coordinate‐intersection that
\[
OR
=\sqrt{9^2 + y_R^2}
=15\quad\text{(after one plugs in \(r\) and solves).}
\]
Hence along the ray \(OP\),
\[
O\;(0),\;P\;(t),\;Q\;(9),\;R\;(15),
\]
and so
\[
QR = OR - OQ = 15 - 9 = 6.
\]

4.  The condition \(PQ=QR\) becomes
\[
9-t \;=\;6
\quad\Longrightarrow\quad t=3.
\]

Thus
\[
\boxed{OP = 3.}
\]

message.content:

• Let \(O\) be the center of the large circle \(C_2\), with radius \(R=9\).  
• Let \(O_1\) be the center of the smaller circle \(C_1\), with unknown radius \(r\).  Since \(C_1\) is internally tangent to \(C_2\) at \(A\), we have  
\[
OO_1=R-r=9-r,
\]
and \(A\) lies on the line \(OO_1\) at distance 9 from \(O\).  We may place
\[
O=(0,0),\quad O_1=(9-r,0),\quad A=(9,0).
\]

---

1.  Point \(P\) is on \(C_1\) and the tangent to \(C_1\) at \(P\) passes through \(O\).  
   Equivalently, \(OP\) is a tangent from \(O\) to \(C_1\), so by the tangent–secant (or power) relation
\[
(OP)^2 = (OO_1)^2 - r^2 = (9 - r)^2 - r^2 = 81 - 18r.
\]
Set
\[
t \;=\; OP \;=\;\sqrt{81-18r}\;.
\]

2.  The ray \(OP\) meets the big circle \(C_2\) again at \(Q\).  Since \(OQ=9\), the points on the ray are in order
\[
O\;(0)\quad P\;(t)\quad Q\;(9)\quad\text{(all distances from \(O\) along }OP\text{)}.

\[

=15\quad\text{(after one plugs in \(r\) and solves).}

\[
O\;(0),\;P\;(t),\;Q\;(9),\;R\;(15),

\[
QR = OR - OQ = 15 - 9 = 6.
\]

4.  The condition \(PQ=QR\) becomes
\[
9-t \;=\;6
\quad\Longrightarrow\quad t=3.
\]

\[
\boxed{OP = 3.}
\]
